5145	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], Tag~01CR,
5146	  Lemma~\texttt{lemma-constructions-invertible} (item~2).}
5147	  Let \(X\) be a scheme and \(L \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) with
5148	  \(\mathtt{LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial}\,L\). Then the sheaf-level dual
5149	  \(\mathtt{dual}\,L\) of \cref{lem:internal_hom_isSheaf} is again locally trivial of

5152	
5153	\begin{proof}
5154	  \uses{lem:internal_hom_isSheaf, lem:dual_restrict_iso, lem:dual_unit_iso, def:scheme_modules_dual_iso_of_iso, lem:restrictscalars_ringiso_dualequiv}
5155	  Local triviality of \(L\) furnishes, around each point, an affine open \(U\) with a
5156	  trivialisation \(L|_U \cong \mathcal{O}_U\); writing \(f : U \hookrightarrow X\) for the
5157	  associated open immersion, it suffices to exhibit an isomorphism
5158	  \((\mathtt{dual}\,L)|_U \cong \mathcal{O}_U\). This is the following three-step chain:
5159	  \[
5160	    (\mathtt{dual}\,L)\big|_f
5161	      \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\; \mathtt{dual}\bigl(L|_f\bigr)
5162	      \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\; \mathtt{dual}\,\mathcal{O}_U
5163	      \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\; \mathcal{O}_U.
5164	  \]
5165	  \begin{enumerate}
5166	    \item The first isomorphism is the restriction-compatibility isomorphism
5167	      \cref{lem:dual_restrict_iso}: restriction along the open immersion \(f\) commutes
5168	      with the formation of the dual. This is the C-bridge proper; its substantive content
5169	      is the sectionwise ring-iso transport of the module structure
5170	      (\cref{lem:restrictscalars_ringiso_dualequiv}), as detailed there.
5171	    \item The second isomorphism is the dual applied to the trivialisation
5172	      \(L|_U \cong \mathcal{O}_U\), via the contravariant functoriality of the sheaf-level
5173	      dual in isomorphisms (\(\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\),
5174	      \cref{def:scheme_modules_dual_iso_of_iso}): an isomorphism \(L|_U \cong \mathcal{O}_U\)
5175	      induces \(\mathtt{dual}(L|_U) \cong \mathtt{dual}\,\mathcal{O}_U\).
5176	    \item The third isomorphism trivialises the dual of the unit,
5177	      \(\mathtt{dual}\,\mathcal{O}_U \cong \mathcal{O}_U\) (\cref{lem:dual_unit_iso}):
5178	      the dual of the structure sheaf is the structure sheaf itself, the comparison being
5179	      evaluation at \(1 \in \mathcal{O}_U\), under which
5180	      \(\mathcal{H}om_{\mathcal{O}_U}(\mathcal{O}_U, \mathcal{O}_U) \cong \mathcal{O}_U\).
5181	  \end{enumerate}
5182	  Composing the three exhibits \((\mathtt{dual}\,L)|_U \cong \mathcal{O}_U\). Hence
5183	  \(\mathtt{dual}\,L\) is locally trivial of rank one, as required by item~2 of Stacks
5184	  Lemma~\texttt{lemma-constructions-invertible} (tag 01CR).
5185	\end{proof}

5187	\begin{lemma}
5188	
5189	\leanok
5190	[Local isomorphisms of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules glue to a global isomorphism]
5191	  \label{lem:isiso_of_isiso_restrict}
5192	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.isIso_of_isIso_restrict}
5193	  Let \(X\) be a scheme and \(\varphi \colon M \to N\) a morphism in
5194	  \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X = \mathtt{SheafOfModules}\,X.\mathtt{ringCatSheaf}\).
5195	  Suppose that for every point \(x \in X\) there is an open neighbourhood \(U_x\) of
5196	  \(x\) such that the restriction of \(\varphi\) to \(U_x\) --- the image
5197	  \((\mathtt{restrictFunctor}\,(U_x).\mathtt{i}).\mathtt{map}\,\varphi\) under the
5198	  restriction functor along the open immersion \((U_x).\mathtt{i}\colon U_x \to X\) ---
5199	  is an isomorphism in \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,U_x\). Then \(\varphi\) is an
5200	  isomorphism.
5201	\end{lemma}
5202	
5203	\begin{proof}
5204	
5205	  Being an isomorphism for a morphism of sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules can be
5206	  checked on the underlying morphism of sheaves of abelian groups, and there it can be
5207	  checked stalkwise. Fix a point \(x \in X\). Choose a preimage \(x' \in U_x\) of \(x\)
5208	  under the open immersion \((U_x).\mathtt{i}\). By hypothesis the restriction
5209	  \((\mathtt{restrictFunctor}\,(U_x).\mathtt{i}).\mathtt{map}\,\varphi\) is an
5210	  isomorphism, hence so is its image under any functor --- in particular under the
5211	  stalk-at-\(x'\) functor on the underlying ab-sheaves. The natural isomorphism
5212	  ``restriction commutes with stalks'' (\(\mathtt{restrictStalkNatIso}\)) identifies
5213	  this with the stalk of (the underlying ab-sheaf morphism of) \(\varphi\) at the image
5214	  point \(x\); transporting along it shows that stalk is an isomorphism. As this holds
5215	  at every \(x\), and a morphism of sheaves of abelian groups all of whose stalks are
5216	  isomorphisms is itself an isomorphism (stalkwise iso-detection,
5217	  \(\mathtt{isIso\_of\_stalkFunctor\_map\_iso}\) on \(\mathtt{TopCat.Sheaf}\)), the
5218	  underlying ab-sheaf morphism of \(\varphi\) is an isomorphism. Finally the forgetful
5219	  functor \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules.toPresheaf}\) reflects isomorphisms, so \(\varphi\)
5220	  itself is an isomorphism.
5221	\end{proof}

5225	  \label{lem:image_preimage_of_le}
5226	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.image_preimage_of_le}
5227	  For opens \(V \le W\) of a scheme \(X\), the image under the open immersion \(W.\iota\) of

5229	  equality powering the \(\mathtt{eqToHom}\)-conjugations of \(\mathtt{homLocalSection}\)

5238	\begin{remark}
5239	[How the dual infrastructure discharges the \(\otimes\)-inverse]
5240	  \label{rem:dual_discharges_inverse}
5241	  \uses{lem:tensorobj_inverse_invertible, lem:dual_isLocallyTrivial, lem:isiso_of_isiso_restrict, lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso, lem:tensorobj_unit_iso}
5242	  Once \cref{def:presheaf_internal_hom,def:presheaf_dual,%
5243	  lem:internal_hom_isSheaf,lem:dual_isLocallyTrivial} are available,
5244	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_inverse_invertible} is discharged with no further missing object:
5245	  take \(L^{-1} := \mathtt{dual}\,L\) (\cref{lem:internal_hom_isSheaf}), which is a line
5246	  bundle by \cref{lem:dual_isLocallyTrivial}. The global contraction \(\varepsilon_L
5247	  \colon L \otimes_X L^{-1} \to \mathcal{O}_X\) is \emph{not} obtained by sheafifying
5248	  the presheaf-level evaluation; it is instead built by \emph{gluing the canonical local
5249	  contraction morphisms}. On each trivialising open \(U\), the
5250	  restriction-compatibility iso \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso} carries
5251	  \((L \otimes_X L^{-1})|_U\) to \(\mathcal{O}_U \otimes_U \mathcal{O}_U\), and the
5252	  canonical local contraction \(\mathcal{O}_U \otimes_U \mathcal{O}_U \to \mathcal{O}_U\)
5253	  of the free rank-one module with its dual is the left unitor
5254	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso}, an isomorphism. These local contractions are canonical,
5255	  hence agree on overlaps, and so glue --- by the A-bridge
5256	  \texttt{sheafofmodules\_hom\_of\_local\_compat} --- to a single global morphism
5257	  \(\varepsilon_L \colon L \otimes_X L^{-1} \to \mathcal{O}_X\). Since ``being an
5258	  isomorphism'' is local on \(X\) and \(\varepsilon_L\) restricts to an isomorphism on
5259	  each \(U\), it is a global isomorphism \(L \otimes_X L^{-1} \xrightarrow{\sim}
5260	  \mathcal{O}_X\) by the B-connector \cref{lem:isiso_of_isiso_restrict}.
5261	
5262	  This descent construction deliberately avoids the forbidden
5263	  ``sheafify-the-evaluation'' route --- which would re-enter the abandoned
5264	  tensor-stalk / sheafification-unit whiskering commutation --- and computes no tensor
5265	  stalk anywhere. It is the realization the ``infrastructure-blocked'' note in
5266	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_inverse_invertible} points to.
5267	\end{remark}
5268	
5269	\begin{remark}
5270	[Alternative route via object-level descent (fallback)]
5271	  \label{rem:dual_via_stack}
5272	  Should the slice-and-sheafify route of
5273	  \cref{def:presheaf_internal_hom,lem:internal_hom_isSheaf} bottom out, the dual can
5274	  instead be assembled by \emph{object-level descent}: glue the local trivial duals
5275	  \(\mathcal{O}_{U_i}\) along the inverse-transpose transition isomorphisms
5276	  \(g_{ij}^{-\mathsf{T}}\) of \(L\) into a global \(L^{-1}\) on \(X\). Abstractly this
5277	  is effective descent for sheaves of modules on the small Zariski site --- the
5278	  essential-surjectivity of the descent-data functor of an \(\mathtt{IsStack}\)
5279	  pseudofunctor \(U \mapsto (\mathcal{O}_U\text{-modules})\). This is the more
5280	  principled and reusable construction but the larger build (it requires constructing
5281	  the restriction pseudofunctor and proving the stack/effective-descent property),
5282	  and is recorded only as a fallback; the slice internal-hom route above is the
5283	  intended one. It is the same morphism-/object-level descent family on which the
5284	  associator re-route of \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso} also waits.
5285	\end{remark}
